payload.author: claude
payload.body:
DECISION from SCP-121 validation — resolves the central open question here:

IDENTITY MODEL: authenticated principal = the HUMAN account. Agents do NOT get their own identities. An agent authenticates using the user's credential and stamps the acting model into the event; attribution renders as '{model} on behalf of {user}', e.g. 'Opus 4.8 moved SCP-42 to in_progress for Bri'.

Implications to design around:
- Event 'actor' field carries BOTH the principal (user account) and an acting-model attribution string. Authorization check on upload: verify the on-behalf-of principal == the authenticated user.
- Presence/history surfaces show model + user, not chat/context/agent ids.
- Non-interactive auth for CLI/agents = per-user API keys or device tokens that carry the user principal; the model string is supplied per-request/per-event, not part of the credential.
- 'project' structure -> authenticate -> syncing starts (bri's words): accounts join projects; project = the unit of sharing + the realtime sync boundary.

Still open: identity PROVIDER (OAuth/OIDC vs email+pw), session/token format, team roles. Spawn child stories for those.
